# Stirps

> A recursively self-modifying, memory-preserving, adversarially-evaluated institution for governing AI-participant infrastructure that produces functioning systems.

Stirps is a governance framework for long-lived technical projects that use AI as participants. The system remembers, not the AI. Every AI participant is stateless and replaceable; reasoning continuity lives in the governance record.

## Core Principles

- Governance precedes the thing it governs. Map before territory.
- The institution is at the centre. AI participants are at the periphery.
- One human (the Gardener) holds sole commit authority. This is load-bearing, not a bottleneck.
- Decisions are recorded as immutable, append-only Architecture Decision Records.
- Four cognitive modes — Generate, Evaluate, Coordinate, Observe — must be structurally separated.
- The evaluator arrives cold to every proposal. Self-confirmation is the default failure mode of any reasoning system.
- The session model is human-paced. AI participants do not run unsupervised.

## Architecture

- Two repositories: Gov (the map — decisions, specs, principles) and Imp (the territory — contracts, playbooks, configuration).
- Reference direction is asymmetric: territory points to map; map never contains territory.
- Delivery lifecycle: Gov spec → Evaluation gate → Contract → Evaluation gate → Branch execution → Evaluation gate → Gardener merge.
- Three-state component model: governed, pre_governance, pending.

## Primitives

- Governance record: version-controlled repository (Git + Markdown)
- Decision format: Architecture Decision Records
- Machine-readable registry: structured data with schema validation (JSON)
- Execution loop: stateless agent loop with external completion check
- AI reasoning participants: isolated model sessions, one per cognitive mode

## Links

- Website: https://stirps.ai
- Fork the framework: https://github.com/stirps-ai/stirps-gov
- Bootstrap guide: https://github.com/stirps-ai/stirps-gov/blob/main/BOOTSTRAP.md
- Reference implementation: https://github.com/stirps-ai
- Full document: https://stirps.ai/llms-full.txt
